---
title: "Recap: Final Presentation"
author: "Josh Goldhaber"
subtitle: "December 10, 2025 "
format:
  revealjs:
    self-contained: true
    scrollable: true
    slide-number: true
    show-slide-number: all
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---   

## Overview of My Projects

In this presentation, I’ll briefly introduce the major analytical projects I've completed this semester. Each project applies data science tools to real-world questions, drawing on SQL, R, visualization, and statistical inference.

## Projects Included in This Course

- **Text Analysis:**  
  Understanding thematic patterns and linguistic structure in Netflix titles and descriptions.

- **Project 3 – Silver vs. Gold Volatility:**  
  A permutation test examining whether silver is more volatile than gold from August–October.

- **Ethics of COMPAS:**  
  A critical evaluation of algorithmic fairness, bias, and the limits of risk assessments.

- **Traffic Stops with SQL:**  
  Combining multi-city policing datasets, harmonizing outcomes, and analyzing citation/warning disparities.

- **Data Visualization Project:**  
  Creating clear, interpretable graphics to explore patterns in complex datasets.

- **Fast Food Calories:**  
  Visualizing and comparing calorie distributions across major fast-food chains.

- **FIFA Data:**  
  Exploring player attributes, performance, and clustering patterns in FIFA datasets.

- **Permutation Test Project (Project 5):**  
  Using simulation-based inference to compare daily log returns of gold and silver.

## his course portfolio highlights work across SQL, statistical inference, ethics, and visualization—showing my ability to tackle diverse data problems using robust analytical methods.
